transcript.text:
Plan:
1. Install any missing Storybook test/runtime dependencies using the Bun lockfile workflow.
2. Replace the default Storybook config with one faithful global preview environment: app CSS, provider tree, seeded browser state, date control, shared MSW handlers, and any needed browser API shims.
3. Write one real story file at a time using existing app patterns, and after each file run `npx vitest --project storybook <story>` until it passes before moving to the next.
4. Finish by cleaning up any obsolete starter stories if needed and run typechecking to catch Storybook-specific typing issues.

I’ve confirmed the shared preview needs a couple of missing packages before we can make the environment realistic and testable, so I’m adding those through Bun and initializing the MSW worker in `public/` to match the project’s lockfile.

transcript.text: I’m patching the shared Storybook environment now: static public serving, global MSW handlers, deterministic wiki fixtures, browser-state seeding, and provider/CSS setup. After that I’ll start adding stories one file at a time and test each one immediately.

The shared runtime is in place. I caught one detail before moving on to the first story file: the Wikipedia handler should match the full URL including query params, so I’m tightening that now instead of discovering it through the first test failure.
I’m creating the first story file with the simplest component so we can validate the new Storybook preview stack early before layering on the provider-backed and fetched-data stories.
The first story file is in. I’m running the Storybook Vitest project against just that file now and will keep iterating until it’s green before adding the next one.

transcript.text: The App story failure turned out to be environment-related rather than story logic: Vite re-optimized `@vercel/analytics/react` during the run, which caused the mid-test reload and the hook errors. I’m locking that dependency into `optimizeDeps.include` so the app stories get a stable React runtime.
I’ve patched the Vite-side cause of that reload. Re-running the App stories now to see what’s left once the runtime stays stable.

content.text:
Plan:
1. Install any missing Storybook test/runtime dependencies using the Bun lockfile workflow.
2. Replace the default Storybook config with one faithful global preview environment: app CSS, provider tree, seeded browser state, date control, shared MSW handlers, and any needed browser API shims.
3. Write one real story file at a time using existing app patterns, and after each file run `npx vitest --project storybook <story>` until it passes before moving to the next.
4. Finish by cleaning up any obsolete starter stories if needed and run typechecking to catch Storybook-specific typing issues.

I’ve confirmed the shared preview needs a couple of missing packages before we can make the environment realistic and testable, so I’m adding those through Bun and initializing the MSW worker in `public/` to match the project’s lockfile.

content.text: I’m patching the shared Storybook environment now: static public serving, global MSW handlers, deterministic wiki fixtures, browser-state seeding, and provider/CSS setup. After that I’ll start adding stories one file at a time and test each one immediately.

File changes:
- update /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-54-06Z-80423e4a/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts
- add /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-54-06Z-80423e4a/project/frontend/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts
- update /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-54-06Z-80423e4a/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx
- add /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-54-06Z-80423e4a/project/frontend/.storybook/wiki-fixtures.ts
- update /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-54-06Z-80423e4a/project/frontend/tsconfig.app.json

The shared runtime is in place. I caught one detail before moving on to the first story file: the Wikipedia handler should match the full URL including query params, so I’m tightening that now instead of discovering it through the first test failure.

File changes:
- update /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-54-06Z-80423e4a/project/frontend/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts
I’m creating the first story file with the simplest component so we can validate the new Storybook preview stack early before layering on the provider-backed and fetched-data stories.

File changes:
- add /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-54-06Z-80423e4a/project/frontend/src/components/Article.stories.tsx
The first story file is in. I’m running the Storybook Vitest project against just that file now and will keep iterating until it’s green before adding the next one.

content.text: I’m adding the page-level stories now. These are the best proof that the global preview is doing the right job, because they combine fetched data, provider state, fixed dates, and the real app shell in one place.

File changes:
- add /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-54-06Z-80423e4a/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx
The page stories are in, and I’m testing them now. If anything in the preview setup is still off, this is the file most likely to expose it.

content.text: The App story failure turned out to be environment-related rather than story logic: Vite re-optimized `@vercel/analytics/react` during the run, which caused the mid-test reload and the hook errors. I’m locking that dependency into `optimizeDeps.include` so the app stories get a stable React runtime.

File changes:
- update /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-54-06Z-80423e4a/project/frontend/vite.config.ts
I’ve patched the Vite-side cause of that reload. Re-running the App stories now to see what’s left once the runtime stays stable.

content.text:
**Changes**
- [.storybook/preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-54-06Z-80423e4a/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx) now mirrors the real runtime: it imports [src/index.css](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-54-06Z-80423e4a/project/frontend/src/index.css), wraps stories in `StrictMode` and `LikedArticlesProvider`, seeds `localStorage`, freezes time with `mockdate`, and applies shared MSW handlers.
